seoTitle: How to Create an Epic in a Jira Kanban Board
metaDescription: Learn how to create an epic on a Jira Kanban board to group related tasks and track larger bodies of work as they move through your process.
intro: Epics let you group related tasks and stories under a larger body of work, which is especially useful for tracking bigger initiatives on a Kanban board. This guide covers creating an epic and connecting issues to it.

steps.heading: Open the epics panel
steps.body: On your Kanban board, find the epics panel, usually accessible from the board's side navigation.

steps.heading: Create a new epic
steps.body: Add a new epic, giving it a name and summary that describes the larger initiative it represents.

steps.heading: Set epic details
steps.body: Fill in additional details like a color to help distinguish it visually or a description outlining the scope of the epic.

steps.heading: Link issues to the epic
steps.body: Assign existing tasks or stories to the epic, or create new ones directly under it, so related work is grouped together.

steps.heading: Track the epic's progress
steps.body: Use the epic panel or a dedicated view to see how much of the linked work has been completed.

faq.question: Can I use epics on a Kanban board the same way as a Scrum board?
faq.answer: Yes, epics work similarly across both board types, serving as a way to group related issues under a larger initiative.

faq.question: How do I add existing issues to an epic?
faq.answer: You can edit an issue's epic link field, or drag and drop it onto the epic from certain views, depending on your Jira setup.

faq.question: Can an epic span multiple projects?
faq.answer: This depends on your Jira configuration, but typically epics are scoped to a single project unless you're using a company-managed setup that supports cross-project epics.

faq.question: How do I know when an epic is complete?
faq.answer: An epic is generally considered complete once all of its linked issues have reached a done status, which most epic views will summarize for you.
